entry.title: Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users – make an impact by joining our CEI project
entry.content:
SAP Customer Engagement Initiatives (CEI) on https://influence.sap.com allow you, as an SAP customer or partner, to gain early insights into SAP's product developments and work directly with SAP teams to shape future product directions. This project presents a unique opportunity to work with SAPUI5 flexibility product managers and engineering teams, leveraging your expertise customizing SAPUI5 apps with key user adaptation.

Join the upcoming CEI project to make an impact on how key users interact with key user adaptation

If you’re already convinced – join here. If not, read on 🙂

What is key user adaptation?

Key user adaptation is a mode that allows extending SAPUI5 applications for other users right in the browser. It is highly intuitive and requires no technical or programming skills. Just use drag and drop to make UI changes – no modification or copy of SAP standard coding necessary! Read more here.

What is the targeted improvement?

Although the product is mature and widely used, we actively evolve it by delivering new functionality and capability enhancements alongside continuous user experience improvements, informed by UI testing and direct customer feedback. 

We are in the process of overhauling key user adaptation’s UI, simplifying the overall approach while making often-used scenarios more streamlined. We’ve taken a close look at the version history (of activated adaptations) and how our users interact with it, extended the “embed third-party content” feature, widened the scope of custom fields that can be added to the UI – and more.

To ensure that we are on the right way, we need your help!

Who should participate?

Users who want to adapt standard SAPUI5 apps for business users. Both key users and technical users are welcome.

Why should you participate?

  • Gain understanding of SAPUI5 flexibility
  • Chance to influence a central SAPUI5 feature
  • Early access to prototypes

How can you participate?

Don’t miss out on this opportunity to share your feedback and engage with SAPUI5 flexibility development teams. Register by March 15, 2026, and make a true impact on how you can customize SAP apps to your needs!

Click here to sign up for our project. (A short overview about how to get started on influence.sap.com can be found here).

After you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call where you will get more information. This call is followed by individual or grouped feedback sessions.

Thank you in advance for helping us shape the future of SAPUI5 flexibility!

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud：增强版 New User Experience Switch 全面解析
entry.content:
在 SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 中，SAP 持续推进用户体验（UX）的革新，其中 New User Experience Switch（新版用户体验切换功能） 是帮助企业顺利完成应用替换的重要机制。最新版本（2602）对该功能进行了进一步增强，让业务用户与管理员更容易适应新旧应用的切换进程。 

         本文将带你了解这个增强功能的核心设计、工作方式，以及企业在实际使用中可以获得的价值。

一、为什么需要 New User Experience Switch？

         SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 每 6 个月发布一次新版本，其中会逐步引入新应用（phase-in），同时也可能逐步淘汰旧应用（phase-out）。通常，业务用户有 至少 12 个月 的窗口期来适应新的替代应用，但在实际项目中，这段时间可能转瞬即逝。 

         为避免用户错过迁移时机，SAP 引入 New User Experience Switch，通过界面提示与切换选项来“温和推动”用户尝试并过渡到新应用。

二、New User Experience Switch 的三种提示阶段

         切换功能在应用生命周期的不同阶段会呈现不同的提示样式：

1. Information 信息阶段

  • 告诉用户有新的应用版本正在被引入
  • 展示新应用的优势
  • 鼓励用户尝试
  • 用户可以随时切回旧应用

         这是“最温和”的阶段，旨在减少用户对改变的抵触情绪。

2. Warning 警告阶段

         当旧应用已经被标记为 deprecated（即将淘汰） 时，切换功能会显示黄色的警告信息：

  • 告知旧应用将在未来版本中不再提供
  • 提醒用户尽快完成迁移

3. Alert 紧急阶段

         这是旧应用被移除前的最后一个版本，切换功能会以更醒目的方式提示：

  • 强调迁移的紧迫性
  • 显示明确的移除时间点

         在这个阶段，如果用户仍然停留在旧应用上，会面临无法继续使用的风险。

三、切换功能如何呈现？

         SAP 在 2502 版本引入 New User Experience Switch，在 2508 与 2602 的版本中进一步增强：

  • 旧应用启动时出现提示 popover（气泡提示）
  • Shell Bar（顶端工具栏）中出现 切换按钮
  • 用户可在新旧应用之间自由切换（取决于阶段）

         这些交互方式让用户无需离开当前体验即可了解版本状态并快速做出选择。

四、对业务用户意味着什么？

✔ 更轻松的学习曲线

不再需要由管理员单独告知新旧应用替代关系，系统会自动提示。

✔ 提前感知变化

避免新版本发布后突然发现旧应用“消失”，有效减少工作中断。

✔ 自主试用新应用

在 phase-in 阶段，用户可以自由在新旧体验中切换，加速熟悉度提升。

五、对管理员意味着什么？

管理员可以利用此功能：

  • 提前规划应用替换节奏
  • 知道哪些用户仍在使用旧应用
  • 在企业层面推动统一迁移

这让应用治理更加可控，也能减少升级周期带来的沟通成本。

六、为什么说这个功能“让改变更容易”？

New User Experience Switch 正是帮助企业完成改变的机制：

  • 让改变更加可见
  • 让迁移更加自主
  • 让用户更有掌控感

对于每一个正在推进数字化、云化的企业来说，这种设计都能有效降低内部阻力。

结语：更智慧、更温和的用户体验演进机制

         New User Experience Switch 的增强不仅让用户更轻松地迈向 SAP Fiori 的现代化体验，也让管理员和企业能够更从容、可控地管理应用生命周期。

         随着 SAP 持续对 UX 的改进（例如 2602 版本中的 AI 辅助、增强的 Launchpad、手机端优化等），未来企业的 SAP 使用体验将更加智能、一致、顺滑。 

         如果你正在规划 S/4HANA Cloud 的上云旅程或准备迎接新版本，这项功能值得你重点关注。

 

参考文档：SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA Cloud – New User Experie... - SAP Community

entry.title: How Do I Activate a Situation Type in the Extended Framework?
entry.content:
Situation Handling notifies users about issues requiring their attention. You may choose SAP delivered situation templates or design your own use cases. For the extended framework for Situation Handling, we often discussed how you can create custom situation templates. The framework comes also with SAP delivered use cases that you can easily activate.

This blog post explains how you can activate situation types from SAP delivered situation templates with the extended framework for Situation Handling. It has been available since SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2202 and SAP S/4HANA 2021 FPS02.

 

SAP Delivered Use Cases

Looking for Situation Handling use cases? Visit SAP Help and navigate to Situation Handling, then choose Use Cases. You can narrow your results by filtering for Situation Template Type (either extended object-based or message-based), area, and topic. To learn more about a specific use case, simply check the link in the Situation Template column for a detailed description.

Use Case

For illustration purposes we use the situation template Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Purchasers get a heads-up for purchase contracts that are about to expire.

 

Activation Overview for 2SL and 3SL

Before discussing the activation steps in detail, let me point out the differences between system landscapes and their specific transport paths.

In the extended framework for Situation Handling, you create a situation type from a situation template. Situation templates are only visible in the quality system of a 2-system landscape (2SL) and the development system of a  3-system-landscape (3SL). The situation type needs to be transported to the production system. The transport paths differ between 2SL and 3SL.

Transport the Situation Type in 2SL

In a 2-system-landscape, you transport the situation type from the quality system to the production system.

  • In the quality system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Export the well-defined situation type
  • In the production system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between quality and production system you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 2-System-Landscape

Transport the Situation Type in 3SL

In a 3-system-landscape, transports are required from the development system to both the test and production system. In a 3SL environment you may also have different tenants for development, customizing, and parallel customizing that need to be considered as well.

  • On the customizing tenant in the development system you
    • Create a situation type from a situation template
    • Export the situation type
  • On the test tenant in the test system you
    • Import the situation type
    • Since the data usually differs between systems you need to enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Test if the configuration alerts the right users about the situation
    • Forward the well-defined situation type
  • On the production tenant in the production system you
    • Import the forwarded situation type
    • Enter the appropriate values for the condition filters
    • Enable the situation type

Transports in 3-System-Landscape

Detailed Activation of a Situation Type in 3SL

Now let’s check the detailed steps for a sample use case in a 3-system-landscape as it is more comprehensive. In 2SL the export / import mechanism resembles the one from development system to test system in 3SL.

Depending on your system setting, the transports may also be processed automatically. For optimal clarity, the blog post discusses the transport steps through manual triggers.

Development System

Create a Situation Type from a Situation Template

Log-in as business process configuration expert on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app where you see a list of SAP delivered templates. Use the search option to find the template for our use case Expiration of Purchase Contracts (PROC_ABOUT_TO_EXPIRE_CTR). Select the template and an object page opens. In the header, select Create Situation Type.

Create situation type from situation template

A new page opens with the situation type that contains all information from the situation template. You just need to enter an ID, Name and Description. The type is configured and ready to use. Depending on your requirements you could adapt the settings, for example, by adding filter values for the conditions, changing texts or rescheduling the batch job.

I recommend monitoring the instances to see how situations are handled.

Monitor situations

Let’s check the relevant settings and understand how the configuration works.

There are two Situation Triggers that detect situations and cover different usage scenarios. While the batch-based trigger checks for situations in the background, the event-based trigger reacts on every active change of the contract.

Situation triggers

The situation triggers cover the following cases:

  • The Event-Based Trigger checks the conditions whenever you create or update a purchase contract.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation. The information displayed to the user is defined in Situation Display number 1.
    • Condition 2 is only executed if condition 1 did not match. That is, the validity period is not within the next 30 days, for instance when the contract got extended, and the situation is closed. As nobody will be informed about the closure, the texts are empty (Situation Display number 2).

Event-based trigger

  • In addition to the events, the Batch-Based Trigger regularly checks the validity end date according to the batch schedule.
    • Condition 1 checks if the validity period of a purchase contract expires within the next 30 days and triggers a situation.

Batch-based trigger

The configuration for the Batch Job Scheduling specifies that the contract dates are verified every 3 hours throughout the day, beginning at 6:00 a.m.

Batch job scheduling

Export the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the customizing tenant in the development system. Open the Export Software Collection app.

  • On top of the table choose Create Software Collection and a new page opens
  • Choose Add Items and select the situation type you created
  • On the header select Check and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Export and wait until the export is finished successfully

Test System

Import the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator on the test tenant in the test system. Open the Import Collection app.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Test the Situation Type

As business process specialist you can test the situation type on the corresponding tenant.

Make sure that the situation type is enabled.

Activate the situation type

Then maintain the conditions. The filter values are not transported since they may differ in the various systems and tenants.

  • Select the Event-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Condition 2 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Event-based trigger

  • Select the Batch-Based Trigger and a new page opens
    • Condition 1 select Edit and enter the filter values
      • Ext. Reference ID: enter Empty, using the value help
      • Validity Period End: enter Next30Days, using the value help
    • Go back to the main page

Batch-based trigger

With the situation triggers, you defined when a situation is detected. Now, you need to define who should be informed about situations. Maintain the team(s) in the Manage Teams and Responsibilities app and ensure that at least one team is enabled. The filter settings work the same way as with the standard framework for Situation Handling.

Situation recipients

Now you can test the situation type in your business area with the corresponding application data. You can check if there are purchase contracts that match the conditions by choosing Trigger Batch and Manage Instances.

Test the type with trigger batch

If no situations were detected you can log-in as purchaser and create or manipulate a purchasing contract. Add an end date within the next 30 days and the save event will trigger a situation.

When you log-in as a team member of the recipients team you can also check, if you were alerted about the situation.

After the successful test you push the situation type to the production system.

Forward the Situation Type

Log-in as administrator and open the Import Collection app.

  • Select your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Forward and wait until the validation is processed successfully
  • Then choose Forward on the bottom and wait until the export is finished successfully

Production System

Import Collection to Production System

As administrator open the Import Collection app on the production tenant in the production system.

  • Choose your software collection and a new page opens
  • On the header select Manual Import and wait until the check is processed successfully
  • Then choose Import on the bottom and wait until the import is finished successfully

Activate the Situation Type

Log-in as business process specialist and open the Manage Situation Types – Extended app. Like in the test system, you need to maintain the situation type.

  • Enable the situation type
  • Add the filter values for the trigger conditions
  • Ensure that at least one recipient team is enabled

Now you are all set.

Further Information

You find many more blog posts in the Best Practices section of Intelligent Situation Handling in the SAP Community. And you can visit SAP Help Portal for further guidance on Situation Handling.

entry.title: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 7: SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud has invested a lot in User Experience, to make it smooth, intuitive and joyful. It supports SAP Business Suite consistency, with the Horizon theme and further updates for navigation, search and more, making it look better and be more intuitive. Users also benefit from the updated home page, AI-assistance, the new My Metrics for staying up-to-date, as well as updates to charts and tables. Story designers also benefit from numerous enhancements to help them produce better content. Finally, we have also made UX enhancements for administrators.

This series of blog posts covers innovations in user experience in our public cloud products from the last six months or so. In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start, along with links to all the other parts.

I’ve grouped the main UX innovations in SAP Analytics Cloud into these sections:

  • Updated home page
  • Design updates for suite consistency
  • AI-assistance
  • Personalizing your experience
  • Chart and table enhancements
  • Helping designers produce better content
  • Enhancements for administrators
  • Further information

 

Updated Home Page

The home page is the first place that users go to when using SAP Analytics Cloud, and hence it is crucial that it provides the most important information, actions and navigation options. Based on thorough user research, we have now redesigned the page to deliver a more streamlined and user-centered experience. At the same time, the new design also supports our focus on providing a consistent user experience across all products within the SAP Business Suite, as described in the next section.

Figure 1 shows an example.

Figure 1: The redesigned home page in SAP Analytics Cloud. Alt Text: screenshot showing a welcome message “Hello, William Yu” at the top, above a page with 7 cards: one filling a quarter of the screen showing learning news, one showing “Just Ask”, one with four quick actions, and four vertical tiles showing lists of files and resources. The left side has a collapsed vertical navigation menu with icons.

Resizable Cards

You can now resize homepage cards vertically, horizontally, or both (depending on card type). For example, you could choose to make the “Learn about SAP Analytics Cloud" card shown in Figure 1 smaller, as depicted in Figure 2.

The ability to resize cards allows you to personalize the home page to suite your priorities and your way of working.

Figure 2: Resizable cards: Alt Text: On the left a wide card “Learn about SAP Analytics Cloud” is shown, with two columns each with text, image and action button, above a section with a link and a search field for documentation. On the right a card “Learn” half as wide, with a carousel for the columns, showing only one column, above the search field.

Cards for Custom Messages and Announcements

Administrators can create custom cards for providing information to all users, such as announcements on upgrades, maintenance windows, or for promoting key content, such as links to training, process updates or new company guidance. These cards can include images, text, tags, and links. Figure 3 shows an example:

Figure 3: Custom card for informing users. Alt Text: A card with an image above a title and two paragraphs of text, followed by two links to more information.

Design Updates for Usability and Suite Consistency

We have made design improvements which are relevant for a consistent user experience across the entire SAP Business Suite, and which are now also available with SAP Analytics Cloud. As I mentioned in my April 2025 blog post Upcoming Design Updates for the SAP Business Suite (SAP Fiori for Web), consistency of the user experience across the various applications used by a user is an important part of overall usability, helping users to feel confident and at ease when using a suite of products – no unexpected surprises.

In this section, we’ll look at these updates:

  • Horizon theme.
  • Further usability improvements (shell bar, navigation menu, user menu, settings, search, illustrations).

Horizon Theme

The visual theme defines the “look” of a product, so I’m glad to say that SAP Analytics Cloud now also provides the Horizon Theme, as shown above in Figure 1. Administrators can switch it on; soon, it will become the default theme. Not only is Morning Horizon available: you will have access to Morning Horizon and Evening Horizon (the light and dark themes) as well as to the high contrast white and black themes that are available for accessibility.

Users can choose which theme they want to use via the new settings UI, as shown in Figure 4 below.

Further Usability Improvements

Shell bar: Figure 1 above already features the new shell bar at the top of the screen, with the product name to the right of the SAP logo.

Navigation: The redesigned navigation bar can be seen on the left side, showing a vertical list of icons representing menu entries for content and tools. Users can hover over each icon to see the name of the module or expand the menu to see all the names shown next to the icons.

User menu (user profile): Figure 4 shows the redesigned user menu. This design is consistent across the SAP Business Suite, with Settings as the first entry, and Sign-Out clearly separated from the other entries.

Unified Settings for Home Page and User Profile: The Homepage settings and User Profile settings are now consolidated and unified in one intuitive interface, which follows the design for consistency across the suite. No need to go to different menus to adjust your personal preferences and homepage layout anymore. Selecting Settings in the user menu brings you to the redesigned settings pop-over. Figure 4 above shows the settings for Appearance, where the user can select the visual theme they would like to use, and Figure 5 below shows the settings for the home screen.

Figure 5: Home Screen settings in the new consolidated settings UI. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the Settings pop-over, with Home Screen selected in the list on the left, and selectors on the right for cards and tools to display on your home screen.

Search: Search now features auto-complete, making it noticeably more convenient and user-friendly. As you begin typing, relevant suggestions appear instantly, streamlining the process of finding models, stories, and files. This enhancement helps you locate what you need more efficiently, even if you can’t recall exact names, ensuring a smoother search experience.

Illustrations: SAP Analytics Cloud has also adopted the new style of illustrations that has been introduced for the whole SAP Business Suite. They are modern, friendly, and visually appealing.

Find out more in this blog post:

  • Introducing Horizon Themes and Usability Enhancements in SAP Analytics Cloud.

 

AI-Assistance

AI can significantly enhance the user experience. In Q3/2025 we introduced these innovations where AI is embedded in the application.

AI-Assisted Commenting

If a table has a lot of comments, especially if some of them are quite lengthy, it can take effort to summarize them all succinctly. Now, with AI-assisted commenting, this is easy. It helps you compile highlights, summarizing data point comment threads, and aggregating data point comments from descendant cells. It will also translate this into your language (specified in your profile). When writing a new comment, you can use AI to help you to rephrase comments. Figure 6 below shows an example of what it looks like in Stories.

Figure 6: AI-assisted commenting, embedded in Stories. Alt Text: Screen snippet showing a report with some visible comments and a pop-over menu with the AI-entry “Summarize Comments of Descendants (English)” selected.

AI-Assisted Chart Summary

Planners and analysts often need to present to and share their findings with managers and colleagues. Summarizing charts in an easy-to-understand way is time consuming – but now much less so, thanks to AI-assisted chart summary. Generate concise, human-readable summaries of SAP Analytics Cloud charts embedded in your PowerPoint presentations at a finger tip, helping you to prepare key take aways faster and communicate more clearly. Figure 7 below shows an example, of an embedded SAP Analytics Cloud data visualization along with an AI powered summary in PowerPoint.

Figure 7: AI-assisted chart summary. Alt Text: Screenshot showing a PowerPoint screen with a chart and generated summary of it on the current slide. On the right side is the SAP Analytics Cloud panel with a preview of the chart and a button to Generate Summary.

Find out more about these, as well as AI-assisted advanced formula generation, in this blog post:

  • Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2025.

 

Personalizing your experience

For business users consuming stories and dashboards in SAP Analytics Cloud, being able to adapt the experience to suit your way of working and provide the information you need, in the way you need it, is crucial for a good user experience. I already covered some personalization above: the resizable cards in the home page, your choice of visual theme, and the other settings you can make via the user menu. However, we have introduced even more powerful personalization:

  • My Metrics, your personalized, curated collection of metrics that matter most to you and your business. 
  • Personalizing data visualization: change the way data is visualized using the new Quick Builder and save the visualization for when you return.

My Metrics

With My Metrics you can choose those metrics that matter most to you and put them together on one screen. These can come from multiple stories. In other words, rather than having to navigate to different stories to get the overview that you want, you can get a personalized overview with My Metrics. You can arrange all your metrics into categorized lists, making it easy to spot trends and understand your data at a glance.

The My Metrics Module

My Metrics was introduced as a new module within SAP Analytics Cloud, you can access it via the left panel from the new Side Menu. My Metrics is broken down into three simple areas:

  1. Your Metrics: A collection of all performance cards you have created or that have been shared with you.
  2. Detailed Visualization: When you select a metric, a chart instantly visualizes its data. The type of chart can be set during the creation of the metric. You can also see exactly which filters are currently applied.
  3. Smart Insights: Go beyond the chart with an automated analysis that reveals hidden patterns and important observations in your data.

Figure 8 shows an example:

  1. On the left, the user has selected the “Gross Margin” metric from their list of metrics.
  2. In the middle, the gross margin is visualized with a horizontal bar chart.
  3. On the right, the smart insights explain what the top contributors to the gross margin are, showing the top location contributor and the top product contributor, with a chart showing the gross margin per product.

Figure 8: My Metrics, showing the three areas described in the main text. Alt Text: Screenshot showing the three columns described in the text, each highlighted in a different color and numbered 1 to 3 from left to right.

Find out more about My Metrics in this blog post:

  • Introducing My Metrics: Your Personalized view of Metrics in SAP Analytics Cloud.

My Metrics Card on Home Page

Now that you can create your own personalized list of metrics, wouldn’t it be great if you could make them available in a card on the home page? The good news is that now you can!

Figure 9 below shows an example, where we have highlighted the My Metrics card with an additional blue border. Clicking on one of the metrics takes you directly to that metric in the My Metrics module: if you for example selected “Gross Margin”, it would take you to the screen shown in Figure 8 above.

Figure 9: The My Metrics card on the home page. Alt Text: Screenshot of the home page with three columns of cards, the rightmost card “My Sales Metrics (9/25)” spanning over the full vertical height and showing five metrics cards.

My Metrics for KPI Monitoring: Alerting

If you want to be alerted when particularly important metrics change, you can do that too: you can subscribe to metrics reports, and set the report frequency (day, week, month). You can also choose to receive reports by e-mail, or through the notification center.

Figure 10: Subscribing to a metrics report. Alt Text: Screenshot of “My Metrics” with a pop-over “Subscribe to Metrics Report” showing a guided procedure with step 1 “General Information” shown, with fields name and description; 2 “Schedule”; and 3 “Distribution List”. “Next” and “Cancel” buttons at the bottom right.

My Metrics also supports collaboration with colleagues: you can share with other users or teams and also use those that they share with you.

To sum up: you get a great user experience with my metrics, since you can personalize the experience to meet your needs: keep an eye on all the metrics that you are interested in, have them front and center on the home page, and ensure that you are notified about changes in ones that you want to track closely.

Personalizing Data Visualization

For you as a consumer of a story, if the way the data is visualized does not suit you perfectly, you can now personalize it yourself – no need to go to the folks who created the stories or dashboards, at least for common cases. The video in Figure 11 shows an example where the user wanted a different representation of the data on career levels shown in the table: with the new Quick Builder, she removed the Headcount column and added the Education Degree dimension, resulting in new rows showing the education degree for each career level.

Figure 11: Using Quick Builder to personalize data visualization. Alt Text: Animated GIF showing two charts above a table “Headcount Analysis”, and then how the user opens the Builder panel and makes the changes to the table described in the text.

 

Chart and Table Enhancements

We have invested a lot in enhancing charts and tables, providing improvement out of the box for all viewers, as well as giving those who build stories and dashboards more options to get an optimal representation. Beyond that, we have also introduced two new charts: an Area Chart and a Pareto Chart.

Table enhancements

By popular demand from customers, we have introduced a number of improvements to the user experience of tables, providing consistency with chart widgets. The enhancements include undo/redo options, mobile compatibility, expanded tooltip settings, improved data synchronization, and a new intuitive builder panel designed for customizing layouts, assigning dimensions, adding calculations, and filtering data.

Find out more in this blog post:

  • Sneak Peek in to SAP Analytics Cloud release for Q3 2025.

Chart enhancements

An enhancement available to all viewers is the new design for the Numeric Point Chart, providing more modern dashboard aesthetics and improving readability. With the fresh look, we ensure key figures stand out more prominently, making it easier for users to quickly grasp performance metrics, as shown in Figure 12.

We have also introduced a number of options for your designers to help viewers better understand the data, as shown in Figure 13 below:

  1. You can add titles to axes, so that it is clearer what data is being represented on each axis.
  2. You can add grid lines to a chart, to make it easier to interpret datapoints at a glance, or if their labels are hidden due to collision.
  3. You can choose how thick you want to make the axis line (the figure shows the “thick” option).
  4. You can choose the color and thickness of the category divider line (the figure shows the “thin” option).

Figure 13: New options for designers to optimize chart visualization. Alt Text: The left shows an example previous line chart, the right shows two versions of the new line chart, with different text orientation of the y-axis title (marked as “1”), and indicators pointing to 2 – 4 as described in the text.

Have a look at these in more detail, as well as some enhancements delivered earlier, in this blog post:

  • Chart Enhancements in SAP Analytics Cloud.

Most recently, we have enhanced the stacked area chart, so that designers can choose a 100% stacked area chart whenever they want to visualize the proportional contribution over time. Figure 14 shows an example, where the proportional contribution barely changes over time.

Figure 14: 100% stacked area chart. Alt Text: An image showing sales for each quarter in 2024, 2025 and 2026, with date on the x-axis, for other (17.6 – 18.0%), food (73.0% - 73.3%) and drink.

Find out more about the 100% stacked area chart here, as well as the new charts covered in the next section:

  • Expanding Visual Analysis: New Area Chart, Pareto Chart, and Stacked Area Enhancements.

New Charts

The new Pareto chart and Area chart help to explain trends, highlight drivers, and support prioritization.

New Pareto Chart

The Pareto Chart is designed to help Story Designers to prioritize focus, explain trade-offs, and guide action instead of treating all contributors as equally important. The chart gets its name from the Pareto principle, which states that often roughly 80% of the outcome comes from 20% of the causes – the “80/20 rule”.

It is very easy for story designers to use: We automatically calculate the Pareto visualization for you based on selected primary measure or account and the dimension. The chart is sorted from highest to lowest, including cumulative percentage which increases to 100%.

Figure 15 shows an example of building a Pareto chart, where the builder has chosen the Account “Days Order to Shipment” and the Dimension “Reasons for Delay”.

Figure 15: Example of the new Pareto Chart, as seen by a story builder. Alt Text: Screenshot showing Builder panel on the left, and an example Pareto Chart on the right.

New Area Chart

The new Area Chart introduces a powerful option for visualizing trends over time while maintaining focus on the overall shape and direction of the data. Compared to Stacked Area Charts, Area Charts emphasize trend and magnitude rather than composition, reduce visual complexity when multiple series are present, and make it easier to follow a single measure’s evolution across time.

Figure 16: Example of the new Area Chart. Alt Text: Image of an area chart for sales in m USD for drink (22.03 – 36.02), food (41.46 – 71.83) and other, with the total 171.99 to 296.11.

Read more about these new charts here:

  • Expanding Visual Analysis: New Area Chart, Pareto Chart, and Stacked Area Enhancements.

 

Helping Designers Produce Better Content

So far, we've focused on UX enhancements elevating overall dashboard and Story consumption. Now, let's also explore how we're enhancing the building experience for our Story designers, making it more intuitive and user-friendly to build dashboards your end-users will enjoy using, with:

  • Design guidelines for designing stories.
  • Built-in performance and usability guardrails.
  • Drag and drop for building data visualizations.
  • Story versioning.
  • New table building experience.

Design Guidelines for Designing Stories

The first step to creating good content is to understand when to use what. Also, if everybody who creates stories and dashboards follows the same guidelines, the overall result will give your users a consistent experience. That is why we have introduced the Dashboard Design Guidelines for SAP Analytics Cloud. Figure 17 shows the overview page, and the page for Insights Formula.

Our aim to assist you in designing dashboards that effectively satisfy the structural, visual, and content-related needs of your end users. Most of the content is tool-agnostic and broadly applicable to the creation of analytical material.

Figure 17: The Dashboard Design Guidelines for SAP Analytics Cloud. Alt Text: Two screenshots, on the left the start page with introduction and six cards for further details, on the right “The Insights Formula” start page for one of the six detail topics. Both images show a navigation menu on the left with links to the Overview and the six topics.

If you are interested in recommendations for dashboard design, have a look at this blog post series:

  • Dashboard Design Best-Practices.

Built-In Performance and Usability Guardrails

Beyond the above guidelines, we have introduced built-in guidance and support for story designers.

Performance Recommendations for Story Designers

The info panel now provides crucial insights into the widgets that influence content performance during the design phase. This enhancement builds upon our existing widget in-app analysis, allowing you to proactively identify areas for improvement and close performance gaps, helping your stories to be both engaging and effective. Read more about it:

  • Performance Recommendations for Story Designers (2025 QRC3)

Consolidated Error Messages in Story Designer

In the past, issues and recommendations were displayed in variuos places, such as individual toast messages or small icons on a widget, whereas performance recommendations were distributed across separate pop-ups or tooltips. This resulted in a fragmented overview, making it easy to miss something.

The new Info Panel consolidates all relevant messages in one location, with three tabs for issues, performance recommendations, and scripting errors. Find out more here:

  • Consolidated Error Messages in SAC Story Designer (2026 QRC1)

Drag and Drop for Building Data Visualizations

Building data visualizations in a story is now easier, since you can use to drag and drop, as shown in the short video in Figure 18. We have also introduced a stand-alone data panel and hit zones on the chart widgets (not visualized in Figure 18).

Figure 18: Drag and drop for building data visualizations in stories. Alt Text: An animated GIF showing the data visualizaton area for the story on the left and the data panel on the right, and the user dragging a measure and then a measure and a dimension from the list on the right to place it on the left, creating a KPI and a chart respectively.

Story Versioning

Designing good stories in SAP Analytics Cloud typically involves a number of iterations, where story designers try out different layouts and narratives, adjust calculations and incorporate feedback from their stakeholders.

If you are doing this kind of iterative work, you’ll often want to go back a step or two and branch out in a different direction. This is where versioning is very helpful – and is now available with the latest release!

Instead of manually creating copies, or painstakingly documenting each change, we have introduced Story Versioning. This is simple by default, offering up to 10 versions per story. Each of these are created when you explicitly request a new version, to which you can add a note to describe it. Any previous version can be opened read-only, and saved as a new story or restored to become the current version. Have a look at Figure 19 to see how the versions are displayed on the right.

Figure 19: Story Versioning. Alt Text: A screenshot of a story, with a “File Versions” panel on the right, showing a vertical timeline with a call-out “Current Version” with a link “Open” above the call-out “Version 1” with links “Restore” and “Delete”.

Find out more in this comprehensive overview:

  • Story Versioning in SAP Analytics Cloud: Design with Confidence & Iterate Without Fear!

New Table Building Experience

The 2025 Q3 release introduced a new Table Builder Panel, bringing the table design process closer to that of charts and geo visualizations. As mentioned above, consistency across applications and products makes it easier for users, since they can use the same work patterns rather than learning different ones. Figure 20 shows the previous UI on the left, and the new one on the right. The main enhancements for the panel are:

  1. Available Object Panel, showing list of available objects
  2. Flexible Totals and Parent Node Positioning
  3. Improved Drop-Down List for Object Selection
  4. New Member Selector
  5. Table Add-Ons
  6. Single Source for Table Styling

Figure 20: New table building experience. Alt Text: Two screen snippets, on the left the previous table builder showing one column, on the right the new experience, with two columns, one for the available objects (new), the other for the builder panel, with four additional features labelled.

We have also introduced a number of enhancements to improve the creation and customization of tabular visualizations, such as:

  1. Ranking & Sorting Improvements (includes Custom Order)
  2. Report to Report Interface Enhancements
  3. Blended Table Improvements
  4. Flexibility with the Version Dimension
  5. Show / Hide Threshold Tooltip Details
  6. Hierarchal and Multi-Selection Primary Structure Input Control
  7. New Client Calculations Panel
  8. Enable Excel Add-in

Read about all of these enhancements to the table building experience in these comprehensive blog posts and documentation:

  • The New Table Building Experience in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Improved Input Readiness and Unbooked Data Display in SAP Analytics Cloud.
  • Documentation: New Table Build Experience.

 

Enhancements for Administrators

We have introduced two new cards for the home page for administrators, making it easier for you to keep an eye on what’s important:

  • Administrative actions card.
  • System monitor card.

Administrative Actions Card

This card proactively pushes information about things that need your attention, providing recommended actions. This saves you time, since you don’t have to go looking, and reduces the time spent on root-cause analysis. Figure 21 shows an example.

Figure 21: Administrative actions card. Alt Text: Image of card with three lines, each with a warning icon, a title and a summary, such as “Classic Stories” – “Conversion needed”, and a link on the right.

System Monitor Card

This card gives you a concise summary of system health, showing key system metrics, user licence health, performance hotspots and governance-related recommendations. As a result, you can spot issues and identify trens quickly, again saving time and making it easier to get your work done. Figure 22 shows an example.

Figure 22: System monitor card. Alt Text: Image of a card showing a carousel of three, with Licensing Information currently displayed: used and unused licences, activated users, and a table “Housekeeping Details” with columns License Type, No Log-On, Inactive, Assigned, Limit.

Find out more about these two cards, as well as the new custom card for informing users that I mentioned at the beginning of this post:

  • What's New on SAP Analytics Cloud Home Screen 2026 Q1 QRC: New Admin Cards.

Further information

To get an overview of all the innovations and enhancements in SAP Analytics Cloud, have a look at these blog posts, video and what’s new documentation:

  • Top 5 Features in Q3 2025.
  • Top 5 Features in Q4 2025.
  • Top 5 Features in Q1 2026.
  • Documentation: What’s New – 2026 Releases.
  • Video: SAP Analytics Cloud: Top 5 New Features – Q1 2026 Release Highlights (8:38 minutes).

 Another good place to stay up to date is the SAP Analytics Cloud page on SAP Community.

 

Continue Reading

I hope you enjoyed this seventh part, covering SAP Analytics Cloud. Here's a link to part eight in a few days, covering latest UX innovations for Customer Experience:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 8: SAP Customer Experience: Mobile Daily Catch-Up for Sales.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 8: SAP Customer Experience: Mobile Daily Catch-Up for Sales
entry.content:
The new Daily Catch-Up within the SAP Sales Cloud mobile app is a real game changer for sales reps, since it directly helps you sell more, by helping you prioritize your day, with a three-step flow highlighting upcoming activities, missed items from the day before, and high-priority deals and leads that otherwise would fall through the cracks. Not only that: it is delightful to use, with nice animations and haptics.

This series of blog posts covers innovations in user experience in our public cloud products from the last six months or so. In case you missed it, Part 1 provides general updates about Joule, SAP Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start, along with links to all the other parts.

 

Revolutionizing the Sales Experience with Daily Catch-Up

Daily Catch-Up is a major user experience innovation designed specifically for mobile sales reps, and is another example of how we are making work form enjoyable, helping users achieve better outcomes with AI.

We’ve learnt from extensive user research and conversations with customers that sales reps often struggle to prioritize their day due to "scattered signals" that surface throughout the day across emails, chats, and various notification channels. The Daily Catch-Up addresses this "notification overload" by unifying these signals into one predictable, guided experience.

Figure 1 gives a first impression: the first two examples show how you can access the catch-up: via a notification, or from the daily summary card on the SAP Sales Cloud apps’s start page. The third example shows the catch-up screen.

Figure 1: Notification and Daily Summary on home screen lead you to your daily catch-up. Alt Text: Three images of mobile phones, as described in the text. The third example shows a card “Visit Kixo Plant” with date and time, and a “Why Prioritized?” section, statint it is starting in 2 hours, with a “Reschedule” and “AI Summary” button and “Mark as Complete”.

Enjoyable: Intuitive and Tactile Design

The new Daily Catch-Up reimagines the interaction model for mobile CRM by moving away from traditional, cluttered lists to a modern, card-based layout.

  • Reduced Cognitive Load: By showing only one card at a time, we keep your attention focused on what matters most without the distraction of competing items. Each card has a clear front and back. A simple tap reveals additional details only when the user needs them, keeping the primary view clean and focussed.
  • Tactile Design: Swiping through cards feels natural, quick, and thumb-friendly, with almost no learning curve. You can use directional swipes to instantly mark tasks as complete or save them for later, without extra taps or navigation.
  • Delightful Experience: Smooth animations and subtle haptics add a touch of delight and give the flow a light, game-like quality. The overall experience feels faster, more intuitive, and more enjoyable on a small mobile screen – as you can see in the animation in Figure 2 below.

Figure 2: Examples of the animations for flipping the card, and for swiping to the next one within a step. Alt Text: Video of a mobile phone, showing how the card rotates around a vertical axis when tapped on, and how you can swipe it upwards towards top right.

Better Outcomes: Heuristic Prioritization and “Top 5 Focus”

We are helping Sales Reps move beyond simple chronological tracking to achieve better business outcomes through smart prioritization.

  • Heuristic Scoring: This new version of Catch-Up uses a scoring system that combines machine learning inputs and local context to calculate a priority score. This ensures Reps see what is most urgent and impactful first.
  • The "Top 5 Focus": Each morning, the system surfaces a "Top 5 Focus" list of prioritized items across opportunities, leads, quotes, orders, accounts or cases that require immediate attention. Ranking follows the same principles of business impact and urgency. Each card includes a short, two-line guidance summary to provide instant clarity.

One Consolidated, Guided Flow

Catch-Up brings everything together, providing a single, predictable way for users to start their day. There are two ways for you to get started: from contextual notifications, or from the app’s homepage, as shown in Figure 3 below:

Figure 3: Contextual notifications and the Daily Summary card on the home page lead you to Daily Catch-Up. Alt Text: Two images of mobile phone. On the left, schematically three notifications are shown on the start screen: “Start Your Day, Focussed”; “You’re at San Francisco International Airport – Do you want to review today’s schedule or follow-ups while you wait?”; “You’re near multiple accounts/leads but don’t have a visit scheduled – Do you want to create a visit?”. On the right the start page, with a Daily Summary card for catch-up, stating “Nice work! You’ve kepe up your Catch-up streak for 3 days straight!”.

Catch-Up has a Three-Step Guided Flow:

  1. Upcoming activities: Highlighting what’s next on the calendar.
  2. Missed items: Covering anything that was overlooked the previous day.
  3. “Top 5 focus”: the high priority items, ranked as described above, that often fall through the cracks because they do not appear in the upcoming activity list.

Figure 4 below shows an animation of how users can move from step 2 to step 3 in the guided flow. You see that on the last card in step 2 you have action buttons to set the status or log a call. Once you swipe it away, you see an example of our new illustration design, with a message saying that CRM is now up-to-date. Clicking on Next Step brings you to step 3, with a prioritized deal. Each card has a “Why Prioritized?” section, explaining why the system has chosen to prioritize this item.

Figure 4: Moving from Step 2 to Step 3. Alt Text: Video of a mobile phone, showing the user swiping up the last card in step 2, getting a message that “Your CRM is now up-to-date – move to next step”, clicking on “Next step” brings up a message “Terry, here are the top 5 things for you to focus on today”, showing a card with the first one.

With Daily Catch-Up, the SAP Sales Cloud mobile app becomes a tool that really helps you sell more, rather than feeling like a tracking tool that your company wants you to use. It becomes a "habit builder" that helps you hit your quotas and stay focused on your real day-to-day goals.

Find out more in the documentation:

  • Mobile: Daily Catch-Up.

Get the SAP Sales Cloud mobile app in one of the app stores:

  • SAP Mobile Sales in Apple app store.
  • SAP Mobile Sales in Google Play.

 

Continue Reading

I hope you enjoyed this eigth part, covering SAP Customer Experience – SAP Sales Cloud. Here's a link to the final part nine, covering latest UX innovations for UI technologies:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 9: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

entry.title: SAP User Experience Q1/2026 Update – Part 9: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile
entry.content:
This ninth and last post in my series gives an overview innovations in our UI tools and technologies, making it ever easier to design, build and integrate UIs, for both web and mobile applications. For designing apps, web and mobile, we have added AI content and more to the SAP Design System portal and UI Kits in Figma. For web developers, we have introduced the SAP Fiori development portal, as well as more AI support via MCP servers for SAPUI5 and for SAP Fiori elements, and Joule integration. Beyond that: have a look at how you can use AI to migrate to TypeScript, learn about the new Geomap control, and benefit from the new SAPUI5 runtime repository. Finally, discover the latest updates for mobile design and development with our SDKs and the mobile development kit.

Joule can also be seen as a technology for delivering a great user experience. To find out about recent innovations there, as well as for central entry points and services, have a look at this post, which also contains links to all the other parts of the series:

  • SAP UX Q3/2025 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

In case you missed them, do have a look at my previous posts covering design and technology news, since what I mention here builds partly on what came before:

  • SAP User Experience Q1/2025 Update – Part 7: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile. This post has a lot of information about extensibility, especially for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Do also have a look at the new SAP Extensibility Explorer.
  • SAP UX Q3/2025 Update – Part 7: UI Design and Technology for Web and Mobile.

First, we’ll have a look at news for creating web UIs, before looking at what’s new for mobile design and development.

 

News for Creating Web UIs

I’ve compiled the news into these sections:

  • SAP Design System Portal and UI Kits – now with AI UI Elements.
  • SAP Fiori Development Portal.
  • Using AI to Develop UIs – MCP Servers, Joule.
  • SAP Fiori Tools News.

SAP Design System Portal and UI Kits – now with AI UI Elements

We recently introduced the new SAP Design System portal that brings together the product design guidelines SAP Fiori for web, SAP Fiori for iOS, and SAP Fiori for Android, as well as to the new digital design system guidelines. With its enhanced usability, simplified versioning and improved performance, designers (and developers!) get a better user experience compared with the previous SAP Fiori design guidelines.

End of last year we introduced these AI UI elements for web UIs, as you can see in Figure 1 below:

  • AI Acknowledgement.
  • AI Button.
  • AI Writing Assistant.

Figure 1: AI UI elements available in SAP Design System portal. Alt Text: Screenshot of the portal UI Elements page with a filter at the top containing “AI”, below that “3 results” showing three cards in a horizontal row for “AI Acknowledgement”, “AI Button” and “AI Writing Assistant”.

Get a complete overview of what’s new in the design system portal here

  • What’s New in the SAP Design System Portal?

UI Kits v8.0 and v9.0 – now with AI components

The new UI Kits available for designers in Figma make it easy for designers to design accessible UIs at enterprise scale, for web applications, mobile iOS and Android applications as well as wearables. The UI Kits provide reusable components for web and mobile, along with web floorplans, which give you what you need out-of-the-box.

In the meantime, we have released SAP Web UI Kit v8.0 which includes a full set of AI components: AI Button, AI Menu Button, AI Split Menu Button, AI Input, and AI Text Area.

The latest release, SAP Web UI Kit v9.0, continues to evolve the system with focused improvements that enhance visual quality, usability, and overall consistency across the kit. It brings:

  • Updated Icons:
    All icons have been refreshed with a 1.5px stroke (down from 1.6px). This improves anti-aliasing and results in cleaner, sharper visuals across interfaces.
  • Busy Indicator Enhancements:
    You now have two Busy Indicator options, static and animated, available on the same page. The new animated version is ready to use out of the box, saving time and implementation effort.
  • Redesigned Color Palette. Note that the previous color palette component has been removed in order to avoid confusion, and keep the kit cleaner and easier to navigate.

Find out more here:

  • New SAP Design System Portal for SAP Fiori Design Guidelines, and New UI Kits for Designers.

SAP Fiori Development Portal

I’m also happy to inform you about the new SAP Fiori development portal. It is designed to help developers maximize the benefits of SAP Fiori elements for OData V4, which is more than just a UI framework: it’s a metadata-driven approach that leverages the SAP Design System as its guiding principle, incorporating the most relevant design system options for typical business tasks.

Figure 2: The SAP Fiori development portal. Alt text: A screenshot showing the Introduction page. On the left a menu has entries Using the Portal, Building Blocks, Global Patterns, Floorplans, SAP Fiori Tools. The Introduction page has a full width card “Building Blocks” at the top, with a description and tiles representing various building blocks. Below that “Choose Your Option” heading above two side-by-side cards for “Floorplans” and “Customer Layout”, each with an image and description, and a “Learn More” button.

The SAP Fiori development portal puts building blocks front and center. These building blocks - such as tables, forms, filter bars, and charts - are orchestrated by SAP Fiori elements and form the basis of every OData V4 application. They provide standardized layouts and interaction patterns, ensuring UI consistency and boosting developer productivity. The great advantage of using building blocks is that you can use them to create a customer layout and yet still benefit from the advantages of the metadata driven approach: low code, built in enterprise-grade capabilities such as multi-language support, accessibility, security and performance, and design system consistency.

Find out more in this six-part series of blog posts, here is the first one, which contains links to the others:

  • Introducing the SAP Fiori Development Portal: Your Gateway to Rapid SAP Fiori App Creation (1 of 6).

We have also restructured our documentation for SAP Fiori elements, with separate entry points for OData V4 and OData V2, and better supporting the building block approach. Read more here:

  • SAP Fiori Elements: New Documentation Structure.

Using AI to Develop UIs – MCP Servers, Joule

The above posts from Q1/2025 and Q3/2025 introduced a number of powerful ways to accelerate UI development by using AI, such as:

  • Creating SAP Fiori elements apps from a sketch (in the Q1 post), now integrated in SAP Build Code and Joule (Q3 post)
  • Joule for SAPUI5 freestyle development (Q1 post)
  • AI-assisted auto-fix of coding errors with UI5 linter (Q3 post)

Recently, we have gone even further and introduced MCP servers for freestyle SAPUI5 development as well as for developing with SAP Fiori elements and the associated building blocks. These open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers provide AI agents with comprehensive UI5 and SAP Fiori elements knowledge, including best practices and project-aware context information, and thus provide developers with valuable support. Figure 3 shows examples.

Figure 3: Examples of the UI5 MCP Server (on the left) and the MCP Server for SAP Fiori (on the right) in action. Alt Text: both images show screenshots of a CLINE console view with a task entered as text at the top, followed by a list of steps which the system proposes to do to solve the task.

Read about these new MCP servers in these blog posts:

  • UI5 MCP Server.
  • MCP Server for SAP Fiori.
  • Setting the right expectations around AI for code generation and getting past the inherent limits

Alternatively, you can listen to these podcasts involving Tim Back and Florian Vogt, talking about the intricacies of the Model Context Protocol (MCP) within the SAP ecosystem:

  • Spotify: UI5 & MCP - Navigating the Model Context Protocol in SAP.
  • Apple Podcast: UI5 & MCP - Navigating the Model Context Protocol in SAP.

Latest UI5 MCP Server Enhancements

The UI5 MCP server got extended to help migrate existing JavaScript code to TypeScript, as well as for generating UI Integration Cards.

New TypeScript Migration Feature

With the newest feature of the UI5 MCP server, UI5-specific TypeScript conversion assistance is now available to AI coding agents, making the task much easier. Find out more in this blog post:

  • UI5 MCP Server's New TypeScript Migration Feature - Real-World Experience and Best Practices

Generating UI Integration Cards

The UI5 MCP Server now supports the development of UI Integration Cards. In its latest version – v0.2.1 – it includes three new tools dedicated to UI Integration Cards:

  1. Best practice guidance for your agent.
  2. Scaffolding a new Integration Card.
  3. Validating the Card’s manifest.

These tools assist Large Language Models (LLMs) in creating new cards from scratch or editing existing ones, following the best practices for card development. Find out more here:

  • UI5 MCP Server Got Extended for UI Integration Cards Generation.

AI News for SAP Fiori Tools and MCP Server for SAP Fiori

The Project Accelerator can now be launched directly from the SAP Fiori generator, streamlining the initial project creation flow. The previously required staging step has been removed, allowing new projects to be created directly in the selected workspace folder. For projects created using the Project Accelerator, the Application Modeler now offers advanced editing capabilities, such as creating new fields and properties end-to-end, updating the data model, exposing the service, and consuming the changes in the UI in one coherent flow.

AI support has also been extended to mock data generation. Developers can generate realistic mock data using AI directly in the Application Modeler, and this capability is available for all applications whenever the Project Accelerator is enabled.

In parallel, the MCP server for SAP Fiori continues to expand. The server now exposes @sap-ux/create commands as CLI functionality, enabling reliable execution in MCP-supported workflows. App generation has been extended to support CAP Java projects, and a new “search_docs” tool allows the LLM (Large Language Model) to query SAP Fiori elements documentation, annotations, SAP Fiori tools documentation, and SAP Fiori Development Portal resources to quickly find relevant guidance and code snippets.

Joule Enhancement: Create Freestyle SAPUI5 Apps in SAP Build Code

You can now use Joule in SAP Build Code to create new freestyle SAPUI5 apps according to your requirements. The feature is not absolutely groundbreaking, as there are other tools doing a similar job like the Easy-UI5 templates and the recently published UI5 MCP Server. But:

  1. It brings the functionality right to the fingertips of developers using SAP Build Code – no other tools needed.
  2. It extends the template capabilities beyond what the Easy-UI5 templates provide.
  3. Implementing it within the Build Code Joule environment yielded interesting insights and further improvements.

Find out more about the feature, and in the second “making of” post, read about how Andreas Kunz went about building it, and what he learnt from doing it:

  • Joule in SAP Build Code can now create freestyle UI5 apps.
  • Inside Joule: Lessons from building an AI coding assistant for SAP Build.

SAP Fiori Tools News

In addition to the AI-enhancements mentioned above, we now provide expanded support for SAP Fiori elements building blocks, a new connection manager for SAP Systems in VS Code, and a feature to preview SAP Fiori web apps on mobile devices.

Expanded Support for SAP Fiori Elements Building Blocks

The following enhancements make it easier to adapt applications and allow developers to work more confidently and efficiently with SAP Fiori elements–based UIs.

Developers can now add more building block types to their apps with the Page Editor, including

  • rich text editors within custom sections,
  • custom columns for table building blocks, and
  • the page building block itself.

Managing custom pages is also more intuitive, as building blocks can now be deleted directly from the editor.

To support experimentation and iteration, undo and redo actions are now available when working on custom pages.

New Connection Manager for SAP Systems in VS Code

We have introduced the Connection Manager for SAP Systems as a dedicated Visual Studio Code extension for managing connections to remote SAP systems. The Connection Manager allows developers to create, store, test, and edit connection information that can be reused by the SAP Fiori generator as well as by preview features in SAP Fiori tools.

Preview SAP Fiori Web Apps on Mobile

When developing SAP Fiori applications, simulating a mobile experience in a desktop browser often isn’t enough to fully validate real-world behavior. Touch interactions, performance characteristics, and device-specific rendering can differ noticeably on physical phones and tablets.

To address this, SAP Fiori tools now supports previewing applications directly on real mobile devices. You do this by using @sap/ux-ui5-tooling version 1.19.1 or higher and creating a new run configuration and enabling Remote Access. When you start the run configuration, the terminal provides connection details, including a URL and a QR code, which can be opened directly on a phone or tablet to access the running app.

Find out more here:

  • SAP Fiori tools 2601 update – Mobile Device Preview, Building Blocks, and AI-Driven Productivity.
  • SAP Fiori Development newsletter January 2026 (issue #38).
  • SAP Fiori Development newsletter issue #39 (March 2026).

 

News for Mobile Designers and Developers

Let’s first look at updates in the design guidelines and the SDKs, and then look at news for the mobile development kit (MDK).

Mobile Design Guideline and SDK Updates

We have introduced numerous new and enhanced patterns and components this last half-year. I have selected a small number to highlight here, further below you’ll find links to get the full overview.

New for AI

The following two new patterns have been introduced for both the SAP Fiori for Android and the SAP Fiori for iOS design guidelines.

AI Insights Pattern: We have introduced a new AI Insights Pattern, which describes how to present AI insights optimally on mobile devices, as shown in Figure 4. It provides a standardized way to display a summary, analysis, or suggestion generated with AI. This pattern can be applied to a full-screen or a card depending on the scope of content generated by AI.

AI Handoff Pattern: We have created a new guideline describing the AI Handoff Pattern, which defines how a user should transition between contextual embedded AI features and the Joule conversational panel.

Figure 4: Two new AI patterns: AI Insights Pattern and AI Handoff Pattern. Alt Text: On the left two image for AI Insights Pattern, first a full mobile phone screen with title and subtitle at the top, followed by two insights sections, each with two slots for content, at the bottom a user feedback row with thumbs up and thumbs down icons, above a an AI generate button. On the right a diagram for the AI Handoff Pattern showing a schematic mobile module with arrow to and from a schematic Joule UI.

Further news

I have picked two stand-out updates, also made available for Android and iOS.

New Illustrations: We have introduced the new illustrations, that were also introduced in the web, to the mobile design system and the mobile SDKs. You can see a few examples on the left in Figure 5 below.

More flexible card layouts: We offer far more flexibility now in how you use cards to summarize information. For example, you can create wider cards that have the image on the left or the right, and the content block next to it. With the new flex item component, the card’s main header can now be customized to hold more content by adding tags and status info labels outside, on top, between, or below the title and subtitle. Two examples are shown in Figure 5 on the right.

Figure 5: New illustrations and more flexible card layout and content options. Alt Text: On the left three different-size “My Daily Tasks” cards, with images for “You’r all caught up”, “Oops” and “Congratulations”. On the right two card examples: at the top a slim wide card with title, subtitle and one line text on the left, image on the right; below that a larger card with image on the left, title and details with colored indicators and buttons on the right.

Find out more here:

  • Introducing SAP Fiori for Android/iOS 25.8: Innovative Enhancements for Android and iOS.

For an overview of all updates, have a look at the what’s new sections in the SAP Design System portal and the SAP Mobile Services Documentation

  • SAP Fiori for Android design: What's New for SAP BTP SDK for Android 26.1 and older.
  • SAP BTP SDK for Android: What’s New in Release 26.1.2 and earlier releases.
  • SAP Fiori for iOS design: What's New for SAP BTP SDK for iOS 26.1 and older.
  • SAP BTP SDK for iOS: What’s New in Version 26.1.2 and earlier releases.

Mobile Development Kit (MDK) News

The Mobile Development Kit, available as part of SAP Mobile Services, enables developers and technical business users to build cross-platform mobile applications. It allows you to build your application once, in an integrated development environment (SAP Business Application Studio / VSCode extension) and run it natively on Mobile Android and iOS devices.  

Here I have chosen to highlight one area: enhancements to the calander control, which now supports date range selection as well as allowing you to highlight special events or statuses directly on the calendar.

The new date range selection displays the calendar in a month-view format and allows multiple months to be shown. Users can scroll vertically through the calendar months. This is ideal if you need to plan events or manage schedules over extended periods, since users can select a time period with a start date and an end date (see Figure 6 below):

  • The first date selected becomes the start date of the range.
  • The second date selected becomes the end date of the range.

Figure 6: Date range selection supported by the mobile development kit provided by SAP Mobile Service. Alt Text: An image of an iPhone and an Android phone next to each other. The iPhone with the light Morning Horizon theme and a date selection Sep 16 – Sep 29, 2025, the other with the dark Evening Horizon theme and a date selection Mon Sep 29 to Fri Oct 17 2025.

Find out more here:

  • Blog post: What’s new in Mobile development kit client 25.9.
  • Documentation: What’s New in Mobile Development Kit 25.9.1 and earlier releases.

 

Further Information

For creating web applications, do register for the upcoming UI5con virtual conference.

Learn more about it at https://openui5.org/ui5con/faq.html, including how to attend, sponsor, or propose a topic for a session.

For links to design and development tools and technology, have a look at the SAP Fiori topic page in the SAP Community, or go directly to these pages:

  • SAP Design System.
  • SAP Fiori tools.
  • SAPUI5.
  • SAP Fiori elements.
  • SAP Mobile technologies.

 

Final words

As you can see from all seven posts in the series, we continue to progress rapidly with the user experience of our product portfolio, especially with regards to AI-assistance.

Part 1 of the series lists all the parts, and links to those parts that have already been published:

  • SAP UX Q1/2026 Update – Part 1: AI, Joule, SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Mobile Start.

Do keep posting your experiences and recommendations yourself in the SAP Community, with the SAP Fiori and/or the User Experience tag! In case you are wondering how to get a list of the most recent blog posts on SAP Fiori and User Experience in our new SAP Community, use these links:

  • Latest blog posts on SAP Fiori in the SAP Community.
  • Latest blog posts on User Experience in the SAP Community.

For general information on design at SAP and SAP Fiori, check out:

  • www.sap.com/design.
  • www.sap.com/fiori.
  • SAP Community: SAP Fiori topic page.

entry.title: AI and UX sessions at SAP Sapphire 2026 that I’m most excited about
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is where SAP makes its big announcements about its vision for the future of ERP and business applications. This includes showing new products, presenting customers who have transformed their business using SAP’s solutions, and highlighting new technologies that will shape the future.

For 2026, AI will continue to dominate the event. AI will change the way that people consume enterprise software through chat-based interfaces and screens that generate on-the-fly based on what you want to do. Eventually.

In this post, I’ll share the sessions at Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference that I’m most excited about. For the full menu, you can browse the session catalog. My preferences are, of course, shaped by my role on the Cloud ERP UX Foundation team. I’ll organize by date so you can plan your agenda. You can also use the browser to search by topic or speaker if that makes it easier for you. Some are even available on-demand. Let’s go!

Monday, May 11, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Monday
10am – 5pm
ASUG102

Unlocking Business Value with AI: Strategy, Security, and the Road Ahead

Gain a comprehensive and practical overview of SAP Business AI. This workshop will examine core strategies, security frameworks, and future trends while exploring real-world customer stories. The session will conclude with an expert panel and a hands-on AI road mapping session.

Many experts from SAP

Monday
2:00pm – 2:20pm
ERP2143

The future of enterprise: Innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private

Discover how SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions enable the next era of enterprise transformation. This introductory session explores how cutting-edge artificial intelligence, trusted data, and integrated applications work in harmony, creating smarter, faster, and more resilient businesses within the security and flexibility of your private cloud.

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Vinay V, SAP

Monday
3:30pm – 3:50pm
PUX1349

Innovations and value that SAP Business AI offers

Learn how you can find the best AI features for your use cases, and understand the business value and associated cost with our self-service tools.

Pascal Mauge, SAP

 

Tuesday, May 12, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Tuesday
10:30 am - 11:15 am PUX1228

Creating better user experiences with AI-assisted SAP Fiori apps | PUX1228

New AI features in SAP Fiori apps boost user productivity and speed onboarding. Starting with the “My Home” page and drilling down into typical SAP Fiori apps, see how AI makes it easier to find information, enter data, automate tasks, and make informed business decisions. Explore how conversational interactions make SAP solutions more intuitive.

 

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Tuesday
11:00 am - 11:20 am PUX1346

Great features don’t guarantee success: Experience does

Explore SAP Business Suite in the cloud to discover how you can shape the experience of the product for stellar adoption success.

Daniel Becker, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm FOU1861

ASUG Community Conversation: Artificial intelligence

Explore platform strategy, embedded and generative AI use cases, integration patterns, and road map priorities with experts in AI and SAP Business Technology Platform. Discuss architecture, data readiness, and clean core alignment while exchanging lessons learned with peers advancing AI innovation.

John Astill, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm ERP1622

SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA: What’s new, why change, and why now?

 Learn from more than 2,400 cloud ERP projects as we navigate the shift from SAP ERP Central Component (SAP ECC) to SAP S/4HANA. Compare greenfield, brownfield, and selective data transitions to find the right path. Discover the key differences in SAP S/4HANA, the value of moving now, and how to accelerate AI adoption with modern technology.

 

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schulze, SAP

Tuesday
11:30 am - 12:15 pm BTM2345

Why ERP transformations fail: The change management gap

Organizations expect operational efficiency and digital capabilities from their SAP solution transformations, yet some ERP programs fail to realize full value. The missing link is often adoption. See how integrating organizational change management early in the SAP lifecycle reduces resistance, accelerates adoption, and improves business outcomes.

 

Kara Beck, Moog Inc.

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:45 pm ERP1864

ASUG Community Conversation: Cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA

In this interactive ASUG Community Conversation for professionals in cloud ERP and SAP S/4HANA, explore strategies across migration, operation, and innovation—featuring our road maps, clean core and AI insights, and customer lessons learned. Exchange practical guidance with peers and shape future discussions through open dialogue.

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Tuesday
1:00 pm - 1:20 pm JOU2645

The future of enterprise applications and AI experiences

Unlock real value from agentic AI with insights from SAP experts and peers. Explore what transformation means today and how you can scale AI across the business, overcome common challenges, govern risk, and build an AI-first enterprise that drives measurable top- and bottom-line impact.

 

Tuesday
2:00 pm - 2:45 pm ERP1178

2026 and beyond: Discover the latest innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private 

Running your ERP in the cloud allows you to benefit from innovations at your own pace. Explore recent AI and other functional developments planned for the next releases of SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions to help you prepare for adoption, build your road map, and accelerate time to value.

Maura Hameroff, SAP

Rudolf Hois, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm FOU1559

Build enterprise-grade pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform

Unlock the power of autonomous agents with enterprise-grade guarantees. Get technical tips on building pro-code agents on SAP Business Technology Platform that balance innovation with responsibility. Learn to use the Model Context Protocol (MCP) for smooth tool integration while supporting security, ethical AI behavior, and regulatory compliance.

Evgenii Skrebtcov, SAP

Tim Back, SAP

Tuesday
3:00 pm - 3:45 pm ERP1834

Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while keeping a clean core | ERP1834

Extend the capabilities of SAP S/4HANA Cloud to address your business needs. Use AI to generate projects in the ABAP programming language and SAP Cloud Application Programming Model from your business requirements. Build SAP Fiori apps based on your written or graphical description, and use natural language filtering to find information in them.

Katja Zoch, SAP

Sebastian Steinhauer, SAP

Tuesday
3:30 pm - 3:50 pm JOU2646

AI as the new UI

The interface is disappearing, and that's the point. As AI becomes the primary way people interact with enterprise software, the question is no longer how to design better screens but how to deliver better outcomes. Intent replaces navigation, conversation replaces configuration, and the most powerful user experience is the one you barely notice.

 

 

Wednesday, May 13, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am SAP1623

SAP ECC to SAP Business Suite: Migration strategies and cloud insights

Master the direct transition from SAP ERP Central Component to SAP Business Suite using proven best practices. Compare public, private, and on-premises deployments to define key decision drivers. Learn to navigate annual releases, leverage essential migration tools, and identify the evolving skills needed for a successful, seamless cloud journey.

Esther Vrieze, SAP

Bert Schultze, SAP

Wednesday
10:30 am – 11:15 am PUX1819

SAP Fiori in action: How modern UX drives efficiency in cloud ERP

Get an overview of how the SAP Fiori user experience (UX) helps users get their work done more easily and efficiently. See how we are using AI today in applications and with the Joule solution.
